categories: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
title: Multi-Granularity Score-Based Generative Framework Enables Efficient Inverse Design of Complex Organics
comment: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
raw_categories: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing

screen.tldr: Introduces a multi-granularity score-based generative framework for efficient inverse design of complex organic molecules, improving controllability and generation quality.
screen.transfer_note: Multi-scale score-based generation and inverse-design conditioning could inspire symmetry-aware crystal generators for polymorph or molecular-crystal candidate generation.

title: A machine learning framework for screening and band gap prediction of stable cubic halide perovskites for energy applications
comment: Journal of Power Sources

screen.tldr: Machine-learning framework screens stable cubic halide perovskites and predicts band gaps, aiming to accelerate discovery for energy applications.
screen.transfer_note: Perovskite stability screening and structure-property models could transfer to crystal candidate filtering and ranking in MCSP.

title: A passivation-molecule anchoring strategy enables dual-defect passivation at the buried interface to enhance perovskite device performance
comment: Surfaces and Interfaces

screen.tldr: Passivation-molecule anchoring at the buried interface enables dual-defect passivation in perovskite devices, improving performance and likely stability.
screen.transfer_note: Interface-defect passivation strategies and perovskite crystal engineering could inform defect-aware screening and stabilization of molecular crystals.

title: Collection of IR absorption spectra for energetic materials calculated using density functional theory

screen.tldr: DFT-computed IR absorption spectra dataset for energetic materials, useful for characterizing and distinguishing crystal polymorphs and related molecular crystal candidates.
screen.transfer_note: Spectral fingerprints could aid polymorph identification, validation of candidate structures, and surrogate models for crystal ranking.

title: Sequential-temperature atomic layer deposition strategy resolves passivation-densification dilemma in CsPbBr3 perovskite quantum dots
comment: Applied Materials Today

screen.tldr: Sequential-temperature atomic layer deposition improves passivation and densification in CsPbBr3 perovskite quantum dots, enhancing stability and optoelectronic performance.
screen.transfer_note: Perovskite crystal growth and phase-stability control strategies could inform finite-temperature polymorph stabilization and defect-aware ranking workflows.

categories: Solar Energy Materials and Solar Cells
title: Long-term (∼3000 h) in situ XRD investigation of environmental stabilization in hybrid perovskites via trace 2D loading
comment: Solar Energy Materials and Solar Cells
raw_categories: Solar Energy Materials and Solar Cells

screen.tldr: Long-term in situ XRD shows trace 2D additive loading stabilizes hybrid perovskites against environmental degradation over thousands of hours.
screen.transfer_note: Insights on additive-driven phase stabilization could inform polymorph control and stability ranking in hybrid crystal design.

categories: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
title: FragLDM: Fragment-Guided Latent Diffusion Model for 3D Molecular Generation
comment: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
raw_categories: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing

screen.tldr: Introduces a fragment-guided latent diffusion model for generating 3D molecular structures, aiming to improve validity and controllability in molecular design.
screen.transfer_note: Fragment-conditioned 3D diffusion ideas could inspire symmetry-aware crystal generation and polymorph candidate sampling.

categories: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
title: Ziformer: Time Efficient SO(3)-Equivariant Graph Neural Network for Molecular Systems
comment: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
raw_categories: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing

screen.tldr: Introduces Ziformer, a time-efficient SO(3)-equivariant graph neural network for molecular systems, aiming to improve accuracy and efficiency in atomistic modeling.
screen.transfer_note: SO(3)-equivariant architecture and efficiency gains could transfer to MLIPs for crystal relaxation and polymorph ranking.

title: Constructing machine learning potential for uranium oxides: efficient simulation of thermo-mechanical properties
comment: Nuclear Materials and Energy

screen.tldr: Builds a machine-learning interatomic potential for uranium oxides to efficiently simulate thermo-mechanical properties, enabling larger-scale atomistic modeling than direct first-principles calculations.
screen.transfer_note: MLIP training and validation workflow could transfer to crystal-energy ranking and finite-temperature property prediction for molecular crystals.

title: Application and development of a crystal structure prediction workflow for novel materials discovery
comment: Open MIND

screen.tldr: Develops a crystal structure prediction workflow for discovering novel materials, directly aligned with polymorph search and ranking in molecular crystal structure prediction.

title: In situ RbPbI3 filler for high-performance carbon-based perovskite solar cells
comment: Solar Energy

screen.tldr: Uses in situ RbPbI3 filler to improve carbon-based perovskite solar cell performance, likely by modifying crystallization, morphology, and interfacial charge transport.
screen.transfer_note: Insights on perovskite crystallization control and phase stabilization could inform crystal-growth strategies and polymorph engineering in MCSP.

title: Thermal behavior analysis and strategic radiation regulation of the perovskite photovoltaic modules
comment: Solar Energy

screen.tldr: Studies thermal behavior and radiation regulation strategies for perovskite photovoltaic modules to improve operational stability and performance.
screen.transfer_note: Thermal modeling and stability insights could inform finite-temperature treatment and degradation-aware screening of crystalline materials.

title: Optoelectronic performance enhancement of All-Perovskite tandem solar cells through textured structure design
comment: Solar Energy

screen.tldr: Uses textured structure design to improve light management and optoelectronic performance in all-perovskite tandem solar cells.
screen.transfer_note: Texture/structure optimization ideas could inform morphology-aware screening and interface engineering in crystalline materials design.

title: A systematic facet-specific mechanical, photodetection, and DFT analysis of MAPbCl3 perovskite single crystals
comment: Materials Science in Semiconductor Processing

screen.tldr: Facet-resolved experiments and DFT analyze mechanical response and photodetection in MAPbCl3 single crystals, highlighting anisotropy across crystal surfaces.
screen.transfer_note: Facet-specific DFT/anisotropy analysis could inform crystal habit, surface stability, and property ranking in molecular crystal polymorph studies.

title: Improved thermal stability of perovskite solar cells via hole transporting materials design

screen.tldr: Design of hole-transporting materials to improve thermal stability in perovskite solar cells, relevant to crystalline materials optimization rather than direct polymorph prediction.
screen.transfer_note: Materials design strategies and stability screening could transfer to selecting crystal-forming molecules and interfaces for robust polymorphs.

title: Continuous hydrogen-bonded networks dictate high proton conduction in a 3D europium-based metal-organic framework
comment: Inorganic Chemistry Communications

screen.tldr: Reports a 3D europium MOF where continuous hydrogen-bonded networks enable high proton conductivity, linking crystal connectivity to transport performance.
screen.transfer_note: Hydrogen-bond network analysis and crystal-structure–property relationships could inform screening of proton-conducting molecular crystals and MOFs.

title: Ab-initio study of physical and hydrogen storage properties of perovskite-type hydrides XOsH3 (X = Ca, Sr, Ba)
comment: Computational Condensed Matter

screen.tldr: First-principles study of perovskite-type hydrides XOsH3, analyzing structural, electronic, and hydrogen-storage properties for Ca, Sr, and Ba variants.
screen.transfer_note: Perovskite crystal energetics and stability analysis could inform transferable workflows for ranking candidate crystal structures and polymorphs.

title: Machine learning applications in the design, optimization, and manufacturing of perovskite solar cells
comment: Solar Energy

screen.tldr: Review of machine learning methods for designing, optimizing, and manufacturing perovskite solar cells, covering materials discovery, process control, and device performance.

title: Multi architecture simulation and predictive modelling of high efficiency lead free perovskite solar cells
comment: Solar Energy

screen.tldr: Uses multiple machine-learning architectures to model and predict performance of lead-free perovskite solar cells, aiming to identify high-efficiency compositions.
screen.transfer_note: Could transfer as a screening and surrogate-modeling workflow for crystalline materials discovery, especially composition–property prediction in perovskite-like systems.

title: Ground state properties and energy-related applications of Cs₂CaXCl₆ (X = Si, Ge) double halide perovskites: A novel DFT study
comment: Computational and Theoretical Chemistry

screen.tldr: DFT study of ground-state structure and energy-related properties of Cs2CaXCl6 double halide perovskites with X = Si, Ge, focusing on stability and electronic characteristics.
screen.transfer_note: Perovskite stability and structure-property workflows could inform crystal screening and ranking strategies for inorganic crystal candidates.

title: Investigating the hydrogen storage potentials of Li2NaCaH6 double perovskite hydride from first-principles insights
comment: Computational Condensed Matter

screen.tldr: First-principles study of Li2NaCaH6 double perovskite hydride assessing structural, electronic, and hydrogen-storage properties for energy applications.
